A modern IT function requires a continuously evolving skill portfolio…

Skill Domain & Why It Matters Now
Continuous Adaptation Imperative
AI & Agentic
Workflow Design
Ability to design, deploy and govern autonomous AI agents that execute multi-step processes — moving beyond prompt-based GenAI to fully operational agentic systems
Skills must evolve from GenAI prompt engineering (2023+) to full agentic orchestration (2025+) as the marginal cost of action approaches zero
Data Engineering
& Governance
Building and maintaining robust data foundations — pipelines, quality controls, metadata management and enterprise-wide analytics to fuel operational and AI capabilities
As data shifts from reporting asset (2020s) to AI fuel (2025+), governance frameworks and data quality skills must be continuously updated to match new model requirements
Platform & SRE
Engineering
Infrastructure-as-code, automated provisioning and Service Reliability Engineering — enabling lean infra teams to manage demand-responsive, self-healing environments
Cloud-native SRE skills must expand to cover AI infrastructure provisioning — GPUs, vector stores, model endpoints — as agentic workloads place new demands on reliability
Product & Partner
Orchestration
Leading self-managing, functionally-aligned product teams and orchestrating an ecosystem of XaaS vendors and strategic partners to deliver plug-and-play capabilities at pace
Product leadership must shift from project delivery to value stream ownership, continuously rebalancing build/buy/partner decisions as the vendor landscape evolves with AI
Integration &
API Architecture
Designing composable, API-first architectures that enable seamless integration across a growing ecosystem of SaaS, data platforms, AI services and venture-backed capabilities
Integration patterns must expand continuously to incorporate agent-to-agent communication protocols and real-time event-driven architectures as ecosystem complexity grows
Cyber, Risk &
AI Ethics
Managing security, compliance and responsible AI governance across an increasingly automated, agent-driven estate with expanded attack surfaces and growing regulatory scrutiny
Risk frameworks must be refreshed continuously — from cloud security postures to AI model risk, bias controls and autonomous agent oversight as regulatory standards mature rapidly
↻

The Continuous Adaptation Imperative

Just as the IT operating model has evolved from Siloed/Functional through to Agentic AI-Driven (1980s → 2025+), skill portfolios cannot be static. Each technology revolution — Compute, GenAI and now Agentic AI — has redefined the marginal cost of a core activity and rendered prior skill baselines insufficient. IT leaders must institute structured skill sensing, regular reskilling cycles and fluid talent models to ensure capability keeps pace with the rate of technological change.

G
Glanbia
